exports.content: Agentic Verifier is a promising paradigm for agentic reward modeling, delivering substantial improvements over SOTA ORMs and PRMs in both parallel and sequential TTS.
exports.label: agentic_verifier_promising_claim

exports.content: The backward agent performs necessity checking by validating that the solution is grounded in the original problem constraints.
exports.label: backward_necessity_claim

exports.content: Coordinating a forward (sufficiency) agent and a backward (necessity) agent yields a bidirectional, tool-augmented review that is more comprehensive than single-turn or unidirectional verification.
exports.label: bidirectional_design_claim

exports.content: Reformulating verification as a bidirectional, multi-turn, tool-augmented process is novel relative to prior reward-model and TTS literature.
exports.label: bidirectional_novelty_claim

exports.content: The forward and backward agents are synergistic: combining them yields strictly better verification performance than either single-direction variant.
exports.label: bidirectional_synergy_claim

exports.content: Agentic Verifier's BoN accuracy continues to improve as N grows from 32 to 128, particularly on challenging benchmarks like AIME24 (reaching 53.3% at N=128 with the 4B variant), unlike many baselines whose accuracy plateaus or declines.
exports.label: bon_scales_with_n_claim

exports.content: Agentic Verifier achieves state-of-the-art Best-of-N performance across MATH500, GSM8K, Gaokao2023, and AIME24, with the 4B variant outperforming the prior best ORM (Skywork-V2-Llama-8B) on MATH500 by 25.2 percentage points.
exports.label: bon_sota_claim

exports.content: The accuracy gains of Agentic Verifier come at a substantial computational cost (tokens, rounds, latency); forward-only and backward-only variants offer scalable trade-offs for resource-constrained deployment.
exports.label: cost_accuracy_tradeoff_claim

exports.content: The multi-turn deliberative process increases compute cost and presents challenges for real-time or resource-limited deployment.
exports.label: cost_limit_claim

exports.content: The forward agent performs sufficiency checking by validating that each preceding step is a sufficient condition for the subsequent derivation.
exports.label: forward_sufficiency_claim

exports.content: The bidirectional, tool-augmented agentic-verification paradigm generalizes beyond math to competitive code and multi-hop QA reasoning.
exports.label: generalization_claim

exports.content: Existing GenRMs are unreliable in complex domains because they suffer from error propagation and lack external grounding.
exports.label: genrm_unreliable_claim

exports.content: Filtering zero-variance reward groups and using clipped importance ratios with a KL penalty yields a stable GRPO update for verifier training.
exports.label: grpo_stability_claim

exports.content: Agentic Verifier provides high-quality critiques that drive faster convergence in iterative refinement: high Delta-up with low Delta-down in early turns and stable or only slightly declining accuracy in later turns, avoiding the degradation seen with competing verifiers.
exports.label: iter_refine_helpful_claim

exports.content: The capability of the multi-agent forward+backward system can be distilled into a single LLM via the AgentV-RL recipe (synthetic-data SFT followed by GRPO RL).
exports.label: multi_agent_distillable_claim

exports.content: Reliable verification calls for an agentic reward modeling paradigm that uses multi-turn reasoning integrated with external tools.
exports.label: paradigm_shift_claim

exports.content: The Agentic Verifier framework scales positively along both axes available to it: inference-time compute (sampled trajectories) and model size (Qwen3 family).
exports.label: scaling_law_claim

exports.content: Reliance on synthetic verification trajectories may limit generalization to real-world reasoning problems whose distribution is not covered by the synthetic engine.
exports.label: synthetic_data_limit_claim

exports.content: Both agents can iteratively decompose complex solutions into verifiable sub-steps and invoke external tools (e.g., a Python interpreter) for numerical calculation.
exports.label: tool_augmented_capability_claim

exports.content: Both the agentic structure and tool integration contribute additively to verifier performance, with the agentic structure providing the larger share of the gain.
exports.label: tool_contributes_claim

exports.content: Performance depends on the coverage and reliability of external tools, which can bottleneck certain tasks.
exports.label: tool_dependency_limit_claim

exports.content: Each stage of the AgentV-RL recipe contributes a measurable improvement: Train-free > Base, SFT > Train-free, SFT+RL > SFT.
exports.label: training_recipe_claim

exports.content: The efficacy of Test-Time Scaling for LLM reasoning is fundamentally dependent on the reward model (verifier) used to score and select candidate solutions.
exports.label: verifier_role_claim
